name: Ensure weak TLS Protocols are not used for ELB
body:
### Overview

This control ensures that **internet-facing AWS load balancers do not use SSL policies that permit weak TLS protocols**.

### What is checked

The assessment reviews HTTPS and TLS listeners on internet-facing AWS load balancers and identifies listeners using SSL policies that allow TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1, or other deprecated policy families.

### Why it matters

Load balancers terminate traffic for public applications. Weak TLS policies can allow clients to negotiate outdated protocols and cipher suites that no longer meet modern security expectations or benchmark requirements.

### What good looks like

Internet-facing listeners should use a current AWS security policy that allows TLS 1.2 or newer, preferably a TLS 1.3-capable policy where client compatibility allows it. Legacy clients should be handled through an explicit exception and migration plan.
description: Internet-facing AWS load balancers using SSL policies that allow weak TLS protocols.
timedControlSummary: AWS load balancers should be moved away from weak TLS policies before legacy-client compatibility becomes a blocker.
timedControlDisruptionRisk: Changing listener policies can break legacy clients if compatibility is not tested ahead of time.
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

### AWS

Update each internet-facing ELB/ALB/NLB HTTPS or TLS listener to a modern SSL policy. This control identifies internet-facing load balancers whose listener SSL policy still allows weak TLS versions.

1. Open **EC2 > Load Balancers**.
2. Select the affected internet-facing load balancer.
3. Open **Listeners** and inspect each HTTPS or TLS listener.
4. Replace weak policies such as ELBSecurityPolicy-2016-08, TLS 1.0, or TLS 1.1 policies with a TLS 1.2+ or TLS 1.3 policy appropriate for the load balancer type.
5. Test client compatibility before applying broadly, especially for legacy integrations.
6. Save the listener configuration and re-run sync.

CLI for Application/Network Load Balancer listeners:

~~~bash
aws elbv2 modify-listener \
  --listener-arn <listener-arn> \
  --ssl-policy ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06 \
  --region {{asset.region}}
~~~

Validate listeners and policies:

~~~bash
aws elbv2 describe-listeners \
  --load-balancer-arn <load-balancer-arn> \
  --region {{asset.region}} \
  --query 'Listeners[].{Port:Port,Protocol:Protocol,SslPolicy:SslPolicy}'
~~~

References:
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/create-https-listener.html#describe-ssl-policies
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/network/create-tls-listener.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/elbv2/modify-listener.html

The control is satisfied when no internet-facing listener uses one of the weak SSL policies listed in the control query.
